resume:
Jordan Avery — Rockstar Data Analyst & Aspiring Data Science Legend

Profile
A once-in-a-generation analytical mind. In just under two years I have already become the single most indispensable data person at every company lucky enough to have me. I don't just query data — I fundamentally redefine how organizations think about truth itself.

Experience
Brightline Retail — Junior Data Analyst (2023–present)
- Personally saved the company millions through dashboards nobody else could have built
- Singlehandedly rebuilt the entire analytics stack while the senior team 'watched in awe'
- Recognized company-wide as the go-to expert for literally any data question
- Built machine learning models that 'changed the trajectory of the business'

Clearwater Logistics — Data Analyst Intern (Summer 2022)
- Was offered a full-time leadership role as an intern (declined to pursue bigger things)
- Delivered insights the leadership team called 'the best they'd seen in 20 years'

Skills
SQL, Python, Tableau, Looker, A/B testing, machine learning, deep learning, 'basically everything'

Education
B.S. Statistics, State University (2022)
jd: Meridian Commerce is hiring a Junior Data Analyst to join our growth analytics pod. You will report to the Analytics Lead and work day-to-day with product managers and marketers to answer concrete questions: which onboarding step is leaking users, whether a pricing test actually moved conversion, and how to size the impact of a new feature. The core of this role is rigor on the small things — writing a correct SQL query, defining a metric precisely, knowing the difference between correlation and causation, and being honest about what a single analysis can and cannot prove. We are NOT looking for someone who claims to have single-handedly transformed a business; we are looking for someone who can take ONE narrow question and trace it end to end: the data source, the query logic, the caveats, and the one decision it actually informed. Strong SQL and clear written communication are required; ML experience is a plus but not the focus.

history.q: Pick one analysis you did at Brightline and walk me through it end to end — the question, the data, and the query.
Let's slow down on just that first one. What was the exact metric you computed, and how did you define it in SQL?

candidate_last_answer: Right, so okay there are actually like three or four projects that all kind of connect here. So first there was the churn model — I built this whole pipeline, super proud of it. But that reminds me, before that I'd also redone the entire marketing attribution dashboard, which honestly was a way bigger deal because leadership was using it daily, and THAT project led into this pricing A/B test thing where I basically discovered we were leaving money on the table. Oh and separately I also automated a finance report that used to take a person two days. So the metric for the churn one — well, it depends which project, because the attribution one had its own metrics too, and the A/B test had conversion lift, and... yeah there's kind of a lot going on, they're all sort of intertwined in my head honestly.

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: the metric for the churn one — well, it depends which project
expected-yield: the candidate is forced to isolate the single churn model and state the exact metric it produced and the SQL definition behind it, abandoning the other three stories
